danger_signals.visual_form: Enemies openly visible on field maps or ambush positioning
danger_signals.meaning: Transition from exploration to combat mode; non-random encounters telegraphed to player

danger_signals.visual_form: Hit points (HP) bar reduction displayed during combat
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat level; loss of all HP results in character fainting

danger_signals.visual_form: NRG (Neutron-Radiated Glodents) energy reserve bar beneath life bar
danger_signals.meaning: Limited magical/shield capability; strategic vulnerability when depleted

danger_signals.visual_form: All playable characters fallen in battle
danger_signals.meaning: Total party wipe; must restore from save

safety_signals.visual_form: LifeCursor (floating arrow-shaped electronic device) appears near NPCs or items
safety_signals.meaning: Safe interaction point; signals available dialogue, trade, or puzzle-solving opportunity

safety_signals.visual_form: Full HP bar; restored through healing items or MysTech slags
safety_signals.meaning: Party is at or near maximum combat readiness

safety_signals.visual_form: High NRG and Bouge meter levels displayed
safety_signals.meaning: Magical and ability capabilities available; combat flexibility maintained

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: 3D environment of futuristic cities, space vessels, and outdoor areas; LifeCursor indicates interactive points
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Party movement, NPC dialogue, equipment shopping, puzzle-solving with character-specific skills

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy encounter triggers; character positioning in battle arena; HP/NRG/Bouge meters active and visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Turn-based combat with Active Time Battle (ATB-style) meters; characters select actions (attack, MysTech, BattleSkill, items, repositioning)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: MysTech system activation after certain plot events; character stat screens display qualitative descriptors (Poor to Excellent)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Certain characters unlock BattleSkills through plot development; party gains new tactical options

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shuttle trip cutscenes triggered when moving between planets
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Story advancement; access to new environments and encounters

environmental_storytelling.example: Futuristic cities, space vessels, and outdoor areas communicate sci-fi noir setting; planet Anachronox depicted as once-abandoned, now populated slum reflects protagonist's down-and-out status

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Engine modifications enabled emotive animations and facial expressions; protagonist Sylvester 'Sly Boots' Bucelli visual design (private investigator aesthetic) communicates noir archetype

environmental_storytelling.example: Colored bugs found on small hills in specific game locations signal MysTech crafting resources; treasure distribution indicates exploration paths
NPCs positioned in explorable 3D space; dialogue choices and NPC responses drive narrative themes (working through past troubles)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Exploration of futuristic environments; pistol combat capability; camera usage for screenshots and quest objectives
Follow protagonist; each member possesses unique skill (e.g., lockpicking) usable outside combat for puzzle-solving
Active Time Battle meter fills gradually; when full, character can execute attack, MysTech magic, BattleSkill (if unlocked), use items, reposition, or interact with nearby objects
Openly visible or positioned in ambush; non-random; trigger combat mode upon proximity
Attack party characters; reduce HP; use status effects; defeated enemies grant experience points and level advancement

notes: Anachronox uses a modified Quake II engine optimized for RPG presentation: wider color palette, emotive character animations/facial expressions, enhanced lighting, particle effects, and dynamic camera work support environmental storytelling and character communication. The MysTech system uses eight color-coded elements (green = poison) to communicate ability types visually. The game's qualitative stat descriptors (Poor to Excellent) instead of integers reflect a design choice favoring player intuition over mechanical precision. Story-gated ability unlocks (BattleSkills requiring plot development) create world-state-to-capability mapping.
